ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON JAN-03-2013 IN 503, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE. NO APPEARANCE AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: GRANTED PER C.C.P. 36(A) AND (D). TRIAL IS APRIL 29, 2013 AT 11:15AM IN DEPT. 503. LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY MOTIONS IS 04/18/13. PLAINTIFF HAS STIPULATED THAT SUMMARY MOTIONS MAY BE BROUGHT ON SHORTENED NOTICE. SUMMARY MOTIONS MAY BE BROUGHT ON 35 DAYS NOTICE, WITHOUT AN ADDITIONAL TWO DAYS FOR E-SERVICE, WITH OPPOSITIONS DUE 7 COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING AND REPLIES DUE 3 COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING. PLAINTIFF TO MAKE A SETTLEMENT DEMAND ON ALL DEFENDANTS NO LATER THAN 04/05/13. SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IN ROOM 603 AT 1:30PM ON 04/18/13. FACT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS 04/12/13. EXPERT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS 04/26/13. NO LATER THAN 01/31/13, PLAINTIFF MUST PROVIDE A STATEMENT STATING, AS TO EACH DEFENDANT, THE NAME AND CONTACT INFORMATION OF EACH PERSON WITH KNOWLEDGE OF ANY FACTS SUPPORTING ANY OF PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS. NO LATER THAN 01/31/13, DEFENDANTS MUST PROVIDE A STATEMENT STATING THE NAME AND CONTACT INFORMATION OF EACH PERSON WITH KNOWLEDGE OF ANY FACTS SUPPORTING ANY OF DEFENDANT'S AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. ANY PERSON NOT IDENTIFIED ON THE STATEMENTS WILL BE BARRED FROM TESTIFYING AT TRIAL OR PRESENTING A DECLARATION ON A SUMMARY MOTION ABSENT GOOD CAUSE SHOWN. THE PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING ANY OTHER SCHEDULING OR OTHER ISSUE WHICH ANY OF THEM WANT TO HAVE INCLUDED IN THE PREFERENCE ORDER. FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON 03/05/13 AT 1:30PM. NO NEED FOR ANY PARTY TO FILE ANY CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE STATEMENT. PARTIES MAY APPEAR AT THE CONFERENCE BY TELEPHONE. THERE WILL BE A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE WITH THE COURT PURSUANT TO SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULES OF COURT RULE 5 ON 04/29/13 AT 1:30PM IN DEPT. 503. THE PARTIES SHALL COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 5.0 AND PROVIDE A CONFIDENTIAL SETTLEMENT STATEMENT BY 04/26/13 AT 12NOON. ORDER SIGNED AFTER THE HEARING. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, MATTER NOT REPORTED

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS CHRISTINA TREMBLAY, ET AL.'S MOTION TO RE-OPEN EXPERT DISCOVERY

No other appearances. Tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to re-open expert discovery is denied. Plaintiffs failed to demonstrate that they met and conferred on this issue, as required by C.C.P. section 2024.050(a). However even if plaintiffs had sufficiently done so, the considerations enunciated in C.C.P. section 2024.050(b) weigh against this Court exercising its discretion to re-open expert discovery. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Dec-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
B. Gardiner McKleroy of WILSON ELSER MOSKOWITZ EDELMAN & DICKER LLP (415-433-0990), appearing for defendant / moving party Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.

RULING:
DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE DRYWALL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing was previously held on Nov. 12, 2014 and continued to this day. No other appearance and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant Golden Gate Drywall Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' negligence and strict liability causes of action and claim for punitive damages is granted. As to negligence and strict liability, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to submit evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant could be liable for these products-based claims. Specifically, plaintiffs failed to submit evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant was a seller, manufacturer and/or distributor of asbestos-containing products to which decedent was exposed, whether defendant sold and profited from asbestos-containing products to which decedent was exposed and, if defendant sold such products, whether sales of these asbestos-containing products matched or predominated the relative importance of defendant's contractor services. As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to submit clear and convincing evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-13-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Tina Yim of IMAI, TADLOCK, KEENEY & CORDERY, LLP (415-675-7000), appearing for defendant / moving party San Francisco Gravel Company, Inc.
Henry Steinberg of MAUNE; RAICHLE; HARTLEY, FRENCH & MUDD, LLC (800-358-5922), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Thomas Tremblay, Christina Tremblay, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO GRAVEL CO., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant San Francisco Gravel Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. The deposition testimony of Mr. Tremblay, Mr. Nicolai, Mr. Robinson and Mr. Chaney and the declaration of Mr. Templin declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Tremblay was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (fireproofing and joint compound) attributable to defendant. As to defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication, it is denied in its entirety for the same reasons stated above. The only ground upon which defendant moved for each summary adjudication issue, as stated in its Notice of Motion and repeated in its Separate Statement, was identical to the ground upon which it moved for summary judgment. Defendant's objections to the deposition testimony of Mr. Robinson and Mr. Chaney are overruled. Defendant's objections to Mr. Templin's declaration are sustained only in that defendant objected that Mr. Templin did not attach any documents to his declaration. Defendant's objections to plaintiffs' references in their separate statement to portions of Mr. Tremblay's deposition testimony that plaintiffs failed to attach are sustained. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara L. Williams of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-12-2014 Continued To Dec-04-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Gardiner McKleroy of WILSON ELSER MOSKOWITZ EDELMAN & DICKER LLP (415-433-0990), appearing for defendant /moving party Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.
Laurel Halbany of MAUNE; RAICHLE; HARTLEY, FRENCH & MUDD, LLC (800-358-5922), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Thomas Tremblay, Christina Tremblay, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE DRYWALL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Defendant Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to plaintiffs' causes of action for false representation and intentional tort and continued as to plaintiffs' causes of action for negligence and strict liability and claim for punitive damages. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs' claims against it. Further, even if defendant had demonstrated that the defense applies, it is not a complete defense to any of plaintiffs' causes of action but only precludes claims based on failure to warn. Summary adjudication of plaintiffs' causes of action for false representation and intentional tort is granted because plaintiffs state that they waive these causes of action against defendant. The hearing on defendant's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' causes of action for negligence and strict liability and claim for punitive damages will be continued pursuant to plaintiffs' C.C.P. 437c(h) request, which is granted. Plaintiffs demonstrated that facts essential to justify opposition of this issues may exist and explained why they could not already be presented. The parties are to meet and confer. An informal hearing is scheduled on Nov. 14, 2014 at 2:30 p.m. in Department 503 if parties need Court's assistance. Hearing on defendant's motion for summary adjudication is continued to Dec. 4, 2014 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503, supplemental opposition due by 11/24 and supplemental reply due by 12/1. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Barrie Hart of BAY AREA TRIAL REPORTERS, CSR #6954 (Tel: 415-328-6644) (503/TLJ)

Defendant Aderholt Specialty Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication.

APPEARANCES: Laurel Halbany, Esq. of Maune Raichle Hartley French & Mudd, LLC 800/358-5922 for pltfs & opposing parties; Ulla M. Pajala, Esq. of Burnham Brown 510/444-6800 for deft & moving party Aderholt Specialty Company, Inc.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant Aderholt Specialty Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to Issues 1 and 4 and granted as to Issues 2, 3 and 5. As to Issue 1 (duty), defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to submit evidence to negate the duty element from plaintiffs' causes of action. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant owed a duty to Mr. Tremblay, precluding defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to Issue 2 (Strict Liability and False Representation) defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant can be liable under the strict liability and false representation causes of action. Further, plaintiffs did not oppose summary adjudication of their strict liability cause of action. As to Issue 3 (Intentional Tort), defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant can be liable for intentional tort. As to Issue 4 (Premise Owner/Contractor Liability), defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that this cause of action fails even if defendant did not own, lease, manage or operate any of the premises at issue. As to Issue 5 (punitive damages), defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' objections to the declaration of Mr. Aderholt are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Holly Sayers of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13678 (510/451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
CALIFORNIA DRYWALL CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: Laurel Halbany, Esq. of Maune Raichle Hartley French & Mudd, LLC 800/358-5922 for pltfs & opposing parties; Ulla M. Pajala, Esq. of Burnham Brown 510/444-6800 for deft & moving party California Drywall Co.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant California Drywall Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to Issues 1, 2 and 3 and denied as to Issues 4 and 5. As to strict liability (Issue 1), defendant sustained its burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant can be liable under a strict liability theory. As to intentional tort and plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages (Issues 2 and 3), defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant can be liable for intentional tort or punitive damages. Further, plaintiffs did not oppose summary adjudication of these claims as they were not addressed in plaintiffs' opposition. As to all duty-related summary adjudication issues (Issues 4 and 5), defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to submit evidence to negate the duty element from plaintiffs' causes of action Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant owed a duty to Mr. Tremblay, precluding defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs' objections to decedent's deposition testimony and the declaration of Mr. Bowles are overruled. The Court did not consider Mr. Templin's declaration as it's irrelevant to the issues raised by defendant's motion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Holly Sayers of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13678 (510/451-1580) (503/TLJ)

No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted per C.C.P. 36(a) and (d). Trial is April 29, 2013 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day for hearing summary motions is April 18, 2013. Plaintiff has stipulated that summary motions may be brought on shortened notice. Summary motions may be brought on 35 days notice, without an additional two days for e-service, with oppositions due 7 court days before the hearing and replies due 3 court days before the hearing. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on all defendants no later than April 5, 2013. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on April 18, 2013. Fact discovery cut-off date is April 12, 2013. Expert discovery cut-off date is April 26, 2013. No later than January 31, 2013, plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than January 31, 2013, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. The parties to meet and confer regarding any other scheduling or other issue which any of them want to have included in the preference order. Further case management conference on March 5, 2013 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on April 29, 2013 at 1:30pm in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by April 26, 2013 at 12noon. Order signed after the hearing. Judge: Teri L.
